source_refs.html: /Users/toy/English/躺着学/躺着学阅读/7180_Passage 3 Evolutionary Psychology in the Workplace.html

passages.title: Evolutionary Psychology in the Workplace
passages.source_html:
A 


Charles Darwin, the brilliant anthropologist and creator of the theory of evolution, is not normally associated with the modern business world. Nevertheless, Darwinian evolutionary theory is the foundation of a new wave of ideas about human behavior in general and particularly the way people behave in the workplace; these ideas have given the title of evolutionary psychology. Evolutionary psychology revolves around the notion that our brains, like our bodies, have an inherited evolutionary design that has scarcely changed for 10,000 years. As respected evolutionary psychology experts Leda Cosmides and John Tooby comment, our modern skulls house a Stone Age mind. The US biologist Edward O Wilson sees evolutionary psychology as being a discipline which is based on both socio-biology, which is the study of the biological basis of social behavior, and psychology, which is the systematic study of human behavior.


B 


Nigel Nicholson, an organizational psychologist from the London Business School, is a strong supporter of evolutionary psychology and on this subject has published Managing the Human Animal. His book takes the reader on a journey from the Stonc Age plains of the savannah to the modern office，and includes a discussion of Darwinism and behavioral psychology together with a dissection of dysfunctional organizational behavior. It is an effective approach explaining why people behave as they do，particularly at work. Evolutionary psychology is increasingly being cited in management circles，where managers are trying to understand puzzling aspects of human behaviour and by doing so improve the workplace. Nicholson believes that evolutionary psychology can help managers understand what goes wrong in organizational life and what they can do about it.


C 


Nicholson maintains that evolutionary psychology dismisses the long-held assumption that our minds are like blank pages just waiting for culture and experience to write on them and shape our nature. He points out that sophisticated research shows the brain actually houses a store of knowledge when we are born, and now genetic research is establishing there are certain genes that account for abilities, tastes and tendencies. The stored knowledge in the human brain has not changed much since the Stone Age. As Tooby and Cosmides stress, there have not been enough generations for a brain that is well adapted to our post-industrial life to evolve through natural selection.

D 


The evolutionary psychology version of human nature revolves around some key elements which we have inherited from our hunter-gatherer minds. One key element is emotion. Emotion was originally essential to keep early man alive and safe from predators. Emotion was, and continues to be our radar, guiding us throughout today's techno-defined business world. Despite this, the business world emphasizes rational not emotional behaviour, and does not admit the importance of emotion. We still use the emotional part of our minds to make sense other people's behaviour and to create an impression, so we can often be taken in by appearances. This mental predisposition actually works best in small communities (the tribe) not in much larger environments filled with people we barely know (the modern workplace). Our minds naturally try to re-create our ancestral communities with networks of no more than 150 people, where there are clear hierarchies and leaders. As a consequence, it takes very little to trigger people's innate distrust of others because our safety in antiquity depended on supporting our near family and friends whom we valued more than other people.


E 


So what advice does Nicholson have for the corporate world？He thinks that by knowing the reasons for people's behaviour it is possible to mould corporate environments into places that have more chance of working efficiently and being pleasant places to work in. Nicholson admits that not everybody in the business world agrees with his belief in the effectiveness of evolutionary psychology in the workplace. One group that resist the theory of evolutionary psychology is young MBA graduates who are just beginning their careers and feel that evolutionary psychology will make their lives at work more difficult. Older and wiser executives points out that they still tend to cling to the idea of a magic formula to bring people into line with corporate strategy. But that is back-to-front thinking according to Nicholson，who contends that we should be reinventing our business structures not our fundamental human nature.


F 


At the end of his book, Nicholson gives his forecast of what will and will not change in the business world. He believes that most people will still prefer more traditional forms of work and throughout their lives will continue to aim at lifelong status advancement. He also maintains that the line between work and home will be less defined, but that people will prefer traditional working patterns if working from home leaves them isolated from their work community. He doubts that the high-tech ideas of virtual companies will ever be very successful because people will still want to meet each other face-to-face. Nicholson describes his ideal organization in the future: it would be decentralized, with small sub-units: the staff would be from diverse backgrounds and be allowed a high degree of self-determination. New endeavors and creativity would replace systems and rationality. Nicholson acknowledges that there is a long way to go in terms of the translation of his ideas of evolutionary psychology into practical propositions, but he is confident more and more people will come round to his way of thinking.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27 - 31


Choose the correct answer.

27

The writers purpose in the first paragraph is to

正确答案： C. explain the theory of evolutionary psychology.

审题：题目问的是作者在第一段中的目的是什么。

 

定位关键词： first paragraph，purpose

 

对应原文： A段，“Nevertheless, Darwinian evolutionary theory is the foundation of a new wave of ideas about human behavior in general and particularly the way people behave in the workplace; these ideas have given the title of evolutionary psychology.”

 

解题思路： 段落 A 中介绍了达尔文的进化论，随后提到“evolutionary psychology” (进化心理学) 是关于人类行为的新思想浪潮的基础。作者的目的主要是解释进化心理学，并引入该概念。作者并没有提出对达尔文理论的反对，也没有进行不同观点的对比，而是在解释进化心理学的概念和基础理论。

28

In the third paragraph which view about evolutionary psychology matches Nicholson's opinion？

正确答案： D. We inherit ideas and characteristics from our ancestors.

审题：题目问的是与Nicholson的观点相匹配的进化心理学观点是什么。

 

定位关键词：Nicholson's opinion, third paragraph

 

对应原文： C段，“Nicholson maintains that evolutionary psychology dismisses the long-held assumption that our minds are like blank pages just waiting for culture and experience to write on them and shape our nature. He points out that sophisticated research shows the brain actually houses a store of knowledge when we are born, and now genetic research is establishing there are certain genes that account for abilities, tastes and tendencies.”

 

解题思路：原文提到 Nicholson 的观点，Nicholson认为我们的思想并不是一张空白的纸，即进化心理学，他认为我们的大脑天生就储存了一些知识，并且一些特定的基因决定了我们的能力、兴趣和倾向。 这表明我们从祖先那里继承了思想和特征。

29

The writer discusses the key element of emotion in order to

正确答案： B. explain attitudes and actions at work.

审题：题目问的是作者讨论情感这个关键元素的目的是什么。

 

定位关键词： emotion，key element

 

对应原文： D段，“One key element is emotion. Emotion was originally essential to keep early man alive and safe from predators. Emotion was, and continues to be our radar, guiding us throughout today's techno-defined business world. Despite this, the business world emphasizes rational not emotional behaviour, and does not admit the importance of emotion. We still use the emotional part of our minds to make sense other people's behaviour and to create an impression, so we can often be taken in by appearances.”

 

解题思路：原文提到情绪是早期人类生存的关键，它仍然是我们的“雷达”，指引我们在当今以科技为主导的商业世界中航行。 尽管工作世界强调理性行为，但人们通过情感理解和回应他人的行为，情绪有其重要性。 这段话解释了为什么情绪会影响我们在工作场所的感受和行为。总的来说，作者通过讨论情感，解释了我们在工作中的行为。

30

Which of the following does Nicholson predict will happen in the business world？

正确答案： D. Social interaction will remain important to workers.

审题：题目问的是Nicholson对商业世界的预测。

 

定位关键词： Nicholson，prediction

 

对应原文： F段，“He doubts that the high-tech ideas of virtual companies will ever be very successful because people will still want to meet each other face-to-face."

 

解题思路： 原文提到Nicholsona认为尽管人们可以在家工作，但他们仍然会更喜欢传统的协作方式，仍然会想要面对面交流，因为这不会让他们与工作社区孤立。 这表明社会互动将继续对员工很重要，将继续在工作中扮演重要角色。


同义替换：Social interaction = meet each other face-to-face

31

Which of the following is the most suitable title for Reading Passage 3？

正确答案： B. Understanding the origins of workplace behavior

审题：题目问的是最适合的文章标题是什么。


定位关键词： title


解题思路： 在 A 段，文章开头提到达尔文的进化论被用于解释人类行为，特别是工作场所的行为。进化心理学帮助理解人类大脑和行为的进化背景。B 段进一步解释了尼科尔森（Nicholson）的观点，认为进化心理学有助于分析和解决组织内的问题。C、D 段则介绍了进化心理学的关键概念，如情绪对人类生存和职场行为的影响。因此整篇文章讨论的是进化心理学对工作场所行为的理解及其对商业世界的应用，并讨论了进化心理学如何帮助理解工作场所的行为。

文章的重点并不在企业管理的变化上，因此“How successful companies manage change.”是错误选项。文章中并没有讨论对达尔文理论的拒绝，反而是利用达尔文的理论解释职场行为，因此“Darwin's theories rejected by modern management. ”是错误选项。文章并没有主要讨论工业后期的组织需要快速进化，因此“Why post-industrial organizations need to evolve more quickly.”是错误选项。

Questions 32 - 35


Choose YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer, choose NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer, or choose NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

32

Nicholson makes a persuasive argument in his book.

正确答案： A. YES

审题：题目问的是 Nicholson 是否在他的书中提出了有说服力的论点。

 

定位关键词： Nicholson，book

 

对应原文： B段，“His book takes the reader on a journey from the Stonc Age plains of the savannah to the modern office，and includes a discussion of Darwinism and behavioral psychology together with a dissection of dysfunctional organizational behavior. It is an effective approach explaining why people behave as they do，particularly at work. ”

 

解题思路： 原文提到了 Nicholson 的著作《管理人类动物》 (Managing the Human Animal)，并指出该书通过解释人们的行为方式来帮助管理者理解工作场所的问题。作者说明这是一个解释人们行为原因的有效办法，因此可以说Nicholson 在书中提出了一个有说服力的论点。 


同义替换：persuasive argument = effective approach 

33

Tooby and Cosmides believe natural selection through the generations has prepared.

正确答案： B. NO

审题：题目问的是Tooby和Cosmides是否相信自然选择通过几代人的传递为我们做好了准备。

 

定位关键词： Tooby and Cosmides


对应原文： C段，“As Tooby and Cosmides stress, there have not been enough generations for a brain that is well adapted to our post-industrial life to evolve through natural selection.”。


解题思路： 原文提到了 Tooby 和 Cosmides 的观点，即我们的大脑从石器时代开始就没有太大变化。 他们认为我们的大脑还没有进化到足以适应现代工业化后的生活。 这表明他们并不认为我们的大脑已经为现代工作场所做好准备。

34

Our reliance on technology causes emotional problems in the workplace.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题：题目问的是我们对技术的依赖是否导致了工作中的情感问题。

 

定位关键词： reliance on technology，emotional problems

 

对应原文： D段，“Emotion was, and continues to be our radar, guiding us throughout today's techno-defined business world.”


解题思路： 文章并没有明确指出技术依赖是否会导致情绪问题，只提到了情绪在工作场所的重要性，但没有将它与技术联系起来。

35

People today are more trusting than they used to be.

正确答案： B. NO

审题：题目问的是人们今天是否比过去更容易信任他人。

 

定位关键词： people today，more trusting


对应原文： D段，“As a consequence, it takes very little to trigger people's innate distrust of others because our safety in antiquity depended on supporting our near family and friends whom we valued more than other people.”


解题思路：原文提到了在当今时代，我们的大脑会自然地重建我们的祖先社区，我们会因此对他人产生不信任感，并且我们祖先生活在小型社区，我们的安全感来源于支持我们的家人和朋友，天生对他人存有猜疑。 这表明作者并不认为现代人比过去更信任他人。


Questions 36 - 40


Complete the summary using the list of words. Choose the correct word and move it into the gap.

Options (Word Bank):
A. business leaders
B. MBA graduates
C. promotion structures
D. reward strategy
E. magic formula
F. strategic planning
G. back-to-front thinking
H. business environments
I. human nature
36

Nicholson's advice to the corporate world


Nicholson believes that if we know why people act the way they do, we can change so employees will work more efficiently. 

正确答案： H

定位信息：employees, more efficently


对应原文：E段， He thinks that by knowing the reasons for people's behaviour it is possible to mould corporate environments into places that have more chance of working efficiently and being pleasant places to work in.   

 

解题思路：原文指出尼科尔森认为了解人们行为的原因能够改变公司环境，使之更加高效。    


同义替换：why=reasons; act=behaviour; change=corporate

37

Nicholson's ideas are unwelcome to but some executives are more open to what evolutionary psychology says.

正确答案： B

定位信息：Nicholson's ideas, evolutionary psychology 

   

对应原文：E段，One group that resist the theory of evolutionary psychology is young MBA graduates who are just beginning their careers and feel that evolutionary psychology will make their lives at work more difficult. 


解题思路：原文指出年轻的MBA毕业生对尼科尔森的观点持抵触态度。


同义替换：unwelcome=resist；business structures = business environments

38

However, these executives still believe that there is a that will make employees act according to the company's practices.

正确答案： E

定位信息：executives    


对应原文：E段    Older and wiser executives points out that they still tend to cling to the idea of a magic formula to bring people into line with corporate strategy.    


解题思路：原文指出了许多高层管理人员仍然相信存在一种“灵丹妙药” (magic formula) 可以让员工按照公司战略行事。    

39

According to Nicholson, these senior executives are engaging in and

正确答案： G

定位信息：Nicholson， executives    


对应原文：E段    But that is back-to-front thinking according to Nicholson,who contends that we should be reinventing our business structures not our fundamental human nature.    


解题思路：原文指出了 Nicholson 批评高层管理人员的思维方式是“本末倒置” (back-to-front thinking)，他们试图改变人类天性，而不是改变商业结构。    

40

we should not try to change but instead we should change our business structures.

正确答案： I

定位信息：business structures    


对应原文：E段，But that is back-to-front thinking according to Nicholson,who contends that we should be reinventing our business structures not our fundamental human nature.  

  

解题思路：原文指出了 Nicholson 批评高层管理人员的思维方式是“本末倒置” (back-to-front thinking)，他们试图改变人类天性（human nature），而不是改变商业结构。
